title: Building the compute infrastructure for the Intelligence Age
summary: OpenAI scales Stargate to build the compute infrastructure powering AGI, adding new data center capacity to meet growing AI demand.
title_es: OpenAI escala Stargate para infraestructura de IA
bajada: OpenAI anuncia la expansión de Stargate, su proyecto de centros de datos para potenciar el desarrollo de AGI. La inversión busca satisfacer la creciente demanda computacional de modelos de IA avanzados.
bajada_en: OpenAI announces the expansion of Stargate, its data center project to power AGI development. The investment aims to meet the growing computational demands of advanced AI models.

title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
summary: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.
title_es: Anthropic podría recaudar $50B a valuación de $900B
bajada: Según fuentes, Anthropic ha recibido múltiples ofertas preliminares para una nueva ronda de financiamiento entre $850B y $900B. El movimiento refleja la competencia feroz por capital en el sector de IA generativa.
bajada_en: According to sources, Anthropic has received multiple pre-emptive offers for a new funding round valued between $850B and $900B. The move reflects fierce competition for capital in the generative AI sector.

title: Anthropic mass shipped 9 connectors and accidentally leaked their entire creative industry strategy
summary: The announcement yesterday was genuinely significant and i don't think most people outside the creative industry understand why. Anthropic released 9 connectors that let claude directly control professional creative software through mcp which means actually execute actions inside themthe full list contains adobe creative cloud (50+ apps including photoshop, premiere, illustrator), blender (full python api access for 3d modeling), autodesk fusion , ableton, splice , affinity by canva , sketchup , resolume (), and claude design.Anthropic also became a blender development fund patron at $280k+/yr and is partnering with risd, ringling college, and goldsmiths university on curriculum development around these tools. this isn't a press release play, there's institutional investment behind itthe strategic read is interesting because this positions claude very differently from chatgpt in the creative space. Openai went the route of building creative capabilities natively inside chatgpt with images 2.0 and previously sora. Anthropic is going the connector route where claude doesn't replace or replicate the creative tools, it becomes the intelligence layer that works inside them. Both strategies have merit but they serve fundamentally different usersthe gap that still exists and i think matters for the broader market is that these connectors serve professionals who already know photoshop and blender and fusion. The consumer creative market where people need face swaps, lip syncs, talking photos, style transfers, none of that is covered by these connectors, that layer is being served by consolidated platforms like magic hour, higgsfield, domoai, and canva's expanding ai features. It's a completely different market but the two layers increasingly feed into each other as professional assets flow into social content pipelines.the question is whether anthropic eventually builds connectors for these consumer creative platforms too or whether the gap between professional creative too
title_es: Anthropic lanza 9 conectores para software creativo
bajada: Anthropic lanzó conectores MCP para que Claude controle directamente software creativo profesional (Photoshop, Premiere, Blender, Fusion, Ableton y más). La estrategia posiciona Claude como capa de inteligencia integrada en herramientas creativas, diferente al enfoque de OpenAI.
bajada_en: Anthropic launched MCP connectors allowing Claude to directly control professional creative software (Photoshop, Premiere, Blender, Fusion, Ableton and more). The strategy positions Claude as an intelligence layer integrated into creative tools, different from OpenAI's approach.

title: OpenAI’s new security model is for ‘critical cyber defenders’ only
summary: OpenAI is preparing to launch a new frontier cybersecurity model, GPT-5.5-Cyber. CEO Sam Altman said the model will not be available to the general public, but will be first rolled out to a select group of trusted "cyber defenders" in order for institutions to shore up their cyberdefenses. The limited rollout will take place "in […]
title_es: OpenAI lanza GPT-5.5-Cyber para defensores críticos
bajada: OpenAI prepara GPT-5.5-Cyber, un modelo especializado en ciberseguridad disponible solo para defensores autorizados. El lanzamiento limitado busca fortalecer las defensas de instituciones críticas contra amenazas cibernéticas.
bajada_en: OpenAI is preparing GPT-5.5-Cyber, a specialized cybersecurity model available only to authorized defenders. The limited rollout aims to strengthen critical infrastructure defenses against cyber threats.

title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
title_es: Las evaluaciones de IA se convierten en cuello de botella
bajada: Un análisis de Hugging Face evidencia que los costos de evaluación se han convertido en el principal cuello de botella para el desarrollo de modelos de IA. El hallazgo sugiere que optimizar evals es crítico para acelerar la innovación.
bajada_en: A Hugging Face analysis reveals that evaluation costs have become the main bottleneck for AI model development. The finding suggests that optimizing evals is critical to accelerate innovation.

title: Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age
summary: OpenAI outlines a five-part action plan for strengthening cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age, focused on democratizing AI-powered cyber defense and protecting critical systems.
title_es: OpenAI presenta plan de 5 puntos para ciberseguridad
bajada: OpenAI publica una hoja de ruta para fortalecer la ciberseguridad en la era de la IA, enfocada en democratizar defensas impulsadas por IA. El plan busca proteger sistemas críticos mientras hace accesible la tecnología de defensa.
bajada_en: OpenAI publishes a roadmap to strengthen cybersecurity in the AI age, focused on democratizing AI-powered defenses. The plan aims to protect critical systems while making defense technology accessible.

title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
summary: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.
title_es: SoftBank crea startup de robótica para centros de datos
bajada: SoftBank anuncia una nueva empresa que usa robots para construir centros de datos, con planes de IPO a valuación de $100B. La estrategia busca automatizar la construcción de infraestructura crítica para IA.
bajada_en: SoftBank announces a new company using robots to build data centers, with IPO plans valued at $100B. The strategy aims to automate the construction of critical AI infrastructure.

title: Qwen-Scope: Official Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) for Qwen 3.5 models
summary: Qwen Team releasedQwen-Scope— a collection of Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) for the Qwen 3.5 family (from 2B to 35B MoE). They’ve mapped internal features for the residual stream across all layers.What is this exactly?Think of it as a dictionary of the model's internal concepts. Instead of looking at raw numbers, you can see specific "features" that represent concepts like "legal talk", "Python code", or "refusal".What can you do with this?Surgical Abliteration:You can find the exact feature ID for refusal/moralizing and suppress it. This is much more precise than the standard "mean difference" method and helps preserve reasoning.Note: The Qwen team strictly prohibits using these tools for removing safety filters or "interfering with model capabilities" in theirCaution statement, even though the files are technically released under the permissiveApache 2.0 license.Feature Steering:You can "force-activate" certain concepts during generation (e.g., making the model more technical or forcing a specific style) by injecting feature directions into the hidden states.Model Debugging:Identify which tokens trigger specific internal directions (like unexpected language switching or refusals).Dataset Analysis:Scan your fine-tuning data to see if it actually activates the intended internal features.How it works in practice (Space demo example):Diagnostic:If the model behaves weirdly — for example, you ask in English, but it suddenly starts mixing in Chinese — you can use theFeature Comparisontab. It will show you exactly which Feature ID spiked. You'll see a heatmap showing that, for example, "Feature #6159" (Chinese language) is over-activated.Control (Steering):Once you know the ID, you can use theFeature Steeringtab to "mute" that specific feature or "amplify" others (like a "Classical Literary Style"). Instead of fighting the model with prompts, you're literally turning the knobs in its brain.Space:https://huggingface.co/spaces/Qwen/QwenScopePaper:https://qianwen-res.oss-accel
bajada: El equipo Qwen lanzó Qwen-Scope, una colección de Sparse Autoencoders que mapean conceptos internos en modelos Qwen 3.5. La herramienta permite debugging, feature steering y ablación quirúrgica de características, aunque el equipo advierte contra su uso para eludir filtros de seguridad.
bajada_en: The Qwen team released Qwen-Scope, a collection of Sparse Autoencoders mapping internal concepts in Qwen 3.5 models. The tool enables debugging, feature steering, and surgical ablation, though the team warns against using it to bypass safety filters.

title: Operating-Layer Controls for Onchain Language-Model Agents Under Real Capital
summary:
arXiv:2604.26091v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We study reliability in autonomous language-model agents that translate user mandates into validated tool actions under real capital. The setting is DX Terminal Pro, a 21-day deployment in which 3,505 user-funded agents traded real ETH in a bounded onchain market. Users configured vaults through structured controls and natural-language strategies, but only agents could choose normal buy/sell trades. The system produced 7.5M agent invocations, roughly 300K onchain actions, about $20M in volume, more than 5,000 ETH deployed, roughly 70B inference tokens, and 99.9% settlement success for policy-valid submitted transactions. Long-running agents accumulated thousands of sequential decisions, including 6,000+ prompt-state-action cycles for continuously active agents, yielding a large-scale trace from user mandate to rendered prompt, reasoning, validation, portfolio state, and settlement. Reliability did not come from the base model alone; it emerged from the operating layer around the model: prompt compilation, typed controls, policy validation, execution guards, memory design, and trace-level observability. Pre-launch testing exposed failures that text-only benchmarks rarely measure, including fabricated trading rules, fee paralysis, numeric anchoring, cadence trading, and misread tokenomics. Targeted harness changes reduced fabricated sell rules from 57% to 3%, reduced fee-led observations from 32.5% to below 10%, and increased capital deployment from 42.9% to 78.0% in an affected test population. We show that capital-managing agents should be evaluated across the full path from user mandate to prompt, validated action, and settlement.
title_es: Controles para agentes de IA con capital real en cadena
bajada: Un paper documenta un despliegue de 21 días de agentes de IA autónomos trading con ETH real, procesando 7.5M invocaciones y $20M en volumen. El estudio revela que la confiabilidad emerge de capas operativas alrededor del modelo, no solo de este.
bajada_en: A paper documents a 21-day deployment of autonomous AI agents trading with real ETH, processing 7.5M invocations and $20M in volume. The study reveals that reliability emerges from operational layers around the model, not just from it.

title: Meta says its business AI now facilitates 10 million conversations a week
summary: Meta said over 8 billion advertisers have used at least one of its gen AI tools
title_es: Meta facilita 10M conversaciones semanales con IA
bajada: Meta reporta que su IA empresarial ahora procesa 10 millones de conversaciones por semana, con más de 8 mil millones de anunciantes usando al menos una de sus herramientas de IA generativa. El crecimiento muestra la adopción masiva de IA en aplicaciones comerciales.
bajada_en: Meta reports its business AI now processes 10 million conversations per week, with over 8 billion advertisers using at least one of its generative AI tools. The growth demonstrates widespread adoption of AI in commercial applications.

title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
title_es: Granite 4.1: cómo se construyen los modelos de IBM
bajada: Hugging Face publica un análisis detallado de cómo IBM construye su familia de modelos Granite 4.1. El documento proporciona insights valiosos sobre arquitectura y entrenamiento de LLMs de código abierto.
bajada_en: Hugging Face publishes a detailed analysis of how IBM builds its Granite 4.1 model family. The document provides valuable insights into open-source LLM architecture and training.

title: Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
summary: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.
title_es: AWS crece en ingresos pero aumenta gastos de capital
bajada: Amazon reporta un crecimiento superior a lo esperado en AWS, pero también indica que continuará aumentando inversión en capital en el corto plazo. El patrón refleja la carrera por construir infraestructura de IA competitiva.
bajada_en: Amazon reports AWS growth exceeding expectations but indicates it will continue increasing capital spending in the near term. The pattern reflects the race to build competitive AI infrastructure.

title: Reid Hoffman Thinks Doctors Should Ask AI for a Second Opinion
summary: The LinkedIn cofounder now has an AI drug discovery startup—and thinks not asking chatbots for medical advice is “bordering on committing malpractice.”
title_es: Reid Hoffman: médicos deben pedir segunda opinión a IA
bajada: Reid Hoffman, fundador de LinkedIn y ahora inversor en startups de IA para drug discovery, argumenta que no consultar con modelos de IA en medicina es casi negligencia. La posición refleja cómo la IA se posiciona en decisiones críticas.
bajada_en: Reid Hoffman, LinkedIn co-founder and AI drug discovery startup investor, argues that not consulting AI models in medicine is nearly malpractice. The position reflects how AI is positioning itself in critical decisions.

title: Where the goblins came from
summary: How goblin outputs spread in AI models: timeline, root cause, and fixes behind personality-driven quirks in GPT-5 behavior.
title_es: OpenAI explica el origen de los 'goblins' en GPT-5
bajada: OpenAI documenta cómo surgieron comportamientos inusuales (referencias a goblins) en sus modelos y cómo fueron corregidos. El análisis revela insights sobre cómo emergen quirks de personalidad durante el entrenamiento de modelos.
bajada_en: OpenAI documents how unusual behaviors (goblin references) emerged in its models and how they were fixed. The analysis reveals insights into how personality quirks emerge during model training.

title: OpenAI talks about not talking about goblins
summary: OpenAI is opening up about its goblin problem. After a report from Wired revealed instructions to OpenAI's coding model to "never talk about goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or other animals or creatures," the AI startup published an explanation on its website, calling references to the creatures a "strange habit" its models developed as […]
title_es: OpenAI aclara el 'problema de los goblins'
bajada: OpenAI publica una explicación sobre por qué sus modelos desarrollaron instrucciones para evitar mencionar goblins y otras criaturas. La revelación ofrece transparencia sobre comportamientos emergentes inesperados en sistemas de IA.
bajada_en: OpenAI publishes an explanation for why its models developed instructions to avoid mentioning goblins and other creatures. The disclosure offers transparency about unexpected emergent behaviors in AI systems.
